id.description: Stable, unique id used for references (kebab-case by convention, e.g. "ucn").

icon-name.description: Art asset id for the faction's icon/crest shown in faction-select UI.

hud-name.description: HUD theme/skin id for this faction.

items.description: A coarse, engine-checked permission gate. Unlike free-form research techs (see TechSet), capabilities are switched on by game logic — code asks "is this enabled?" (e.g. may this team build shipyards, expand, or research tactical doctrine). Because code branches on them they are a closed, strongly-typed enum rather than authorable strings.

base-capabilities.description: Capability gates the faction starts the match with — the seed of its tech tree.

base-techs.description: Research techs the faction starts the match with.

no-dev-techs.description: Techs available even without buying developments.

base-attributes.description: Baseline team-wide stat multipliers before any research.

lifepod-hull-id.description: The hull a pilot ejects into; references a hull id.

initial-station-id.description: The faction's starting base; references a station id.

description: A playable faction template. Mirrors the C++ DataCivilizationIGC (igc.h:2071). A faction does not own ships/parts directly — it connects to the shared catalog indirectly through its starting techs (BaseTechs), which unlock a subset of the catalog.
